name: cost-spike-explainable
description: Daily cost jumped from $5 to $12. Traced to single user (user-42) running 80 queries vs usual 10. Per-trace cost normal.

item.title: Daily cost anomaly — 140% above baseline
item.description: Automated alert: daily estimated cost reached $12.14, exceeding the $8 threshold (baseline ~$5/day).
item.note:
Investigated the cost spike. Findings:

- Total estimated cost today: $12.14 (vs $5.20 7-day average)
- Breakdown by agent: customer-support-bot $11.40, email-classifier $0.74
- The spike is entirely on customer-support-bot
- Per-trace cost is normal: avg $0.025/trace (vs $0.026 baseline) — no change in model or prompt size
- Trace volume is the culprit: 456 traces today vs ~200/day baseline
- Drilled into user metadata: user-42 generated 80 traces between 09:00-16:00 UTC (vs their usual ~10/day)
- user-42's traces are normal in structure and cost — they're just running many more queries
- Next highest user: user-118 with 22 traces (normal)
- Removing user-42's excess volume: ~376 traces × $0.025 = $9.40, still elevated but closer to baseline
- Actually several other users also slightly above normal — possibly a busy day
- No errors, no quality issues, no prompt changes

Cost spike is explained by a single high-volume user plus generally elevated traffic. Per-trace economics are unchanged. No technical issue.

projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$5
- Trace volume: ~200/day
